title: baeckea cochinchinensis
intro: Baeckea cochinchinensis, also known as the Coastal Baeckea or Wild Quinine, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to alleviate fever, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function. Baeckea cochinchinensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
